summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Resilience Testing Dashboard. 19/19 backend pytest tests pass (one initial assertion-bug in test was fixed). All frontend pages (Login, Register, Overview, Projects, NewTest, TestRun, History) render with all required data-testid attributes wired correctly. End-to-end flow validated: login → create project → DNS verify modal → (admin-verified via mongo) → deploy smoke test against example.com → live charts/metrics update → history shows the run. k6 binary executes successfully and metrics are bucketed per-second into MongoDB. All endpoints enforce auth (cookies), safety caps (5000 RPS / 600s / 2000 VUs), and ownership-verification gate.

minor.issue: When DNS resolver hits NoNameservers/timeout on a clearly non-existent test domain, endpoint can return HTTP 500 instead of the expected 200 with verified=false. Acceptable, but could be wrapped to always return 200 with records_seen=[] for unverifiable hostnames.

issues: Browser console hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' coming from NewTest.js around line 133 — caused by a wrapper span injected into the <option> label. Functional behaviour OK but should be cleaned up (render plain text inside <option>).

action_items: Optional: render plain text (no <span> wrappers) inside <option> in /app/frontend/src/pages/NewTest.js to remove React hydration warning.
Optional: wrap dns.resolver exceptions in /api/projects/{id}/verify to always return 200 with verified=false (currently raises 500 on resolver-level failures).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 779 lines — acceptable for MVP but consider splitting into modules (auth, projects, tests, runner) before scaling.
set_auth_cookies uses secure=False — fine for dev but must be flipped to secure=True (and ideally samesite='none') for production HTTPS deploys.
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' while allow_credentials=True — browsers will refuse credentialed requests with wildcard origin. Production CORS_ORIGINS must be set to explicit URL(s).
K6 subprocess is launched via asyncio.create_task without bounded concurrency — a malicious/over-eager user could DoS the host by triggering many tests in parallel.
No rate-limit / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook expects 5-fail lockout). Not implemented.
Test code & history correctly hide _id (ObjectId serialised to str). No Mongo _id leakage observed in API responses.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@resilience.lab / admin123 (seeded). Test user auto-registered per run with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Tests manually flip verified=true via pymongo (matches test_credentials.md instructions). One verified project on example.com was created under admin during UI test; safe to delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_resilience_backend.py covers auth, projects, stats, safety caps, full k6 smoke run (creates test against example.com, waits ~20s for completion), abort flow. It depends on local mongo (DB_NAME=resilience_db) and creates TEST_-prefixed data. Frontend routes: /login, /register, /, /projects, /tests/new, /tests/:id, /history. All required data-testids present. Note: scenario-*, form-*-slider, new-test-deploy-btn only render when at least one verified project exists for the logged-in user — pre-seed/verify a project before re-testing the deploy flow.
